keywords.keyword: Manual J load calculation online free
keywords.serpWeakness: Top results either require an email sign‑up or are just landing pages for paid software trials; truly free, no‑strings tools are buried.
userComplaints: Every 'free' Manual J tool I found forces me to enter my email before I can see the load calculation.
The free web‑based calculators are just rule‑of‑thumb disguised as Manual J.

keywords.keyword: how many tons of AC do I need for 2000 sq ft
keywords.serpWeakness: Page‑one results give oversimplified '500 sq ft per ton' advice, ignoring climate, insulation and window area; authoritative but nuanced answers are missing.
userComplaints: Every article just tells me X tons per square foot without asking about my home's insulation or windows.
I can't find a calculator that actually walks me through Manual J steps for my exact house.

keywords.keyword: best hvac sizing calculator for homeowners
keywords.serpWeakness: High‑ranking pages are mostly round‑up lists from HVAC contractor sites, not independent tool comparisons; objective, user‑tested reviews are scarce.
userComplaints: Most 'best' lists push their own paid services or refer me to pro software I can't use on my phone.
Nobody tells me which calculator works well on mobile and doesn't demand a contractor account.

keywords.keyword: free hvac load calculator without email
keywords.serpWeakness: Almost all results are lead‑gen tools that gate results behind email submission; a genuinely anonymous, instant‑result calculator would immediately distinguish itself.
userComplaints: I just want a load estimate without handing over my email and getting spammed with quotes.

keywords.keyword: what size heat pump for 2500 sq ft house
keywords.serpWeakness: Answers rely on generic BTU charts; few incorporate heat pump‑specific variables like HSPF rating or cold‑climate performance, leaving an educated gap.
userComplaints: Generic calculators don't ask if I have a heat pump or furnace, and the sizing rules are completely different.

keywords.keyword: btu per square foot cooling calculator
keywords.serpWeakness: Top results are static tables, not interactive tools, and they rarely adjust for sun exposure or ceiling height.
userComplaints: These tables are useless – my living room has a vaulted ceiling and south‑facing glass, and the simple BTU/sqft doesn't cut it.

keywords.keyword: hvac sizing calculator for old homes
keywords.serpWeakness: Very little content specifically addresses loose‑fill insulation, leaky ducts, or single‑pane windows; a dedicated old‑home tool would have virtually no direct competition.
userComplaints: My 1920s house has no wall insulation – how do I account for that in a sizing calculator? Nothing works.

keywords.keyword: how much does manual j calculation cost
keywords.serpWeakness: Only a handful of outdated articles quote vague prices; no clear, up‑to‑date cost breakdowns or free alternatives are easily found.
userComplaints: I want to know if the $299 the contractor quoted for a Manual J is fair, but I can't find reliable pricing info online.

keywords.serpWeakness: Existing free software is poorly maintained (often last updated 2010s) and lacks Windows 10/11 compatibility; reviews aren't up‑to‑date.
userComplaints: The only free Manual J software I found looks like it was built for Windows XP and crashes constantly.

keywords.serpWeakness: Room‑by‑room load calculation tools are almost nonexistent online; most treat the whole house as one block.
userComplaints: I have a bonus room above the garage that's always too hot – how do I size a mini‑split just for that room?

keywords.keyword: best hvac sizing calculator app iphone
keywords.serpWeakness: Only a couple of poorly rated apps exist on the App Store; none combine manual‑J methodology with a clean mobile UI.
userComplaints: There's no decent HVAC sizing app for iPhone – they're either abandoned or full of ads.

keywords.keyword: commercial hvac load calculation spreadsheet free
keywords.serpWeakness: Most spreadsheet downloads come from shady file‑sharing sites; a trusted, openly hosted version with instructions is missing.
userComplaints: I downloaded a 'free commercial load calculation spreadsheet' and my antivirus flagged it. There's nothing safe out there.

keywords.keyword: how to calculate hvac size using manual j
keywords.serpWeakness: Guides are either overly academic (directly from ASHRAE) or too superficial; a practical, step‑by‑step walkthrough for DIYers is rare.
userComplaints: Every tutorial assumes I'm an engineer. I just want a plain‑English walkthrough of Manual J so I can double‑check a contractor's work.

keywords.serpWeakness: Tools that calculate both equipment size and duct sizing simultaneously are almost non‑existent outside expensive pro suites.
userComplaints: Why can't I find one calculator that does the whole house load AND tells me the duct size for each room?
